syllabus.summary: Chemical engineering is a multidisciplinary field that focuses on the economic   production of chemical products through the application of chemical reactions. Beyond   chemistry, it requires a comprehensive understanding of several key areas, including:   Mass/Energy Balance, Reaction Engineering, Fluid Dynamics, Heat and Mass Transfer,   Process Design and Control. This introductory course aims to make these concepts   accessible and easy to understand. It provides a clear understanding of what chemical   engineering entails and is designed to equip students with the fundamental skills   needed to integrate and apply these principles effectively in real-world scenarios.
syllabus.policies: Some engineering calculators may be required.

syllabus.summary: Students will gain hands-on experience in implementing and testing electronic circuits. They will use measurement equipment to build functional circuits on breadboards with passive and active components while developing practical debugging and troubleshooting skills. The final three weeks of the course will be devoted to a creative design project, where students will design, implement, and present a circuit of their own choice.
syllabus.policies: Active discussion and collaboration among students are strongly encouraged. However, all reports must be prepared independently. Any plagiarism or unauthorized use of another person’s work without proper citation will be considered academic misconduct and may result in a substantial reduction in grade.

syllabus.summary: In accordance with the progress of the circuit theory and instrumentation (theory) lecture, experiments are conducted to directly implement and test circuits. Using NI-ELVIS, passive and active components are easily placed on the breadboard to create a circuit with the desired function, and the ability to solve practical problems is developed. In addition, by learning the basics of the LabVIEW program, methods for automating data collection are learned. The last three weeks are devoted to a creative experiment opportunity to create a circuit of one's own choice and present it.
syllabus.policies: Discussion among students is recommended while solving problems

syllabus.summary: 이 강의 시리즈에서는 신경세포의 기본 특성부터 뇌의 구조, 감각 시스템 및 관련 질병에 대해 가르칠 것입니다. 이 과정은 뇌과학을 전공으로 선택하고자 하는 학생들과 뇌 관련 연구 주제에 관심이 있는 학생들에게 강력히 추천합니다.

syllabus.summary: 신경과학 II는 『Neuroscience: Exploring the Brain』에서 Part III “The Brain and Behavior”와 Part IV “The Changing Brain”에 해당하는 내용을 중심으로, 뇌가 어떻게 동기·감정·수면·언어·정신질환 등 고차 행동과 정신 기능을 생성하는지 다루는 과목이다.  화학적 신경조절, 동기와 보상, 성과 뇌, 정서·수면·언어·주의·의식의 신경기전, 정신질환의 신경생물학적 기초를 학습한 뒤, 발달과 가소성에 초점을 맞추어 뇌 배선(wiring), 기억 시스템, 학습과 기억의 분자 기전까지를 폭넓게 다룬다.  강의와 더불어 교과서 내 ‘박스(Box)’ 논문을 학생 발표 및 토론 형식으로 진행함으로써, 최신 연구 결과를 비판적으로 읽고 발표·토의 능력을 기르는 것을 목표로 한다.

Students will learn to design and conduct experiments, analyze data, and interpret results related to key biological mechanisms in the brain. The course emphasizes both theoretical understanding and practical skills in neuroscience research.

syllabus.summary: 인지기능은 외부의 정보를 받아들이고 저장하며, 이를 활용하여 문제를 해결하는 두뇌 고유의 기능을 말하며, 기억력, 집중력, 언어능력, 실행기능, 시공간능력의 5대 영역으로 나누어진다. 두뇌의 인지기능은 정상인 및 환자를 대상으로 한 관찰연구 및 동물실험연구 등의 다양한 방법을 통해 연구되어 왔으며, 지금까지의 연구를 통해 축적된 지식은 뇌영상처리 및 두뇌-컴퓨터 인터페이스와 같은 다양한 첨단의 분야에 응용되고 있다. 본 과목에서는 인간의 인지기능을 구성하는 다양한 요소와, 각 요소를 처리하는 두뇌의 영역에 대해 공부할 것이다.
syllabus.policies: Cell phones should be turned off or set to silent mode during class. No texting or browsing on phones during class. Laptops/tablets should only be used for course-related activities.
textbook: Cognitive Neuroscience (The Biology of the Mind); 6th edition (Michael S. Gazzaniga / n.d.)

syllabus.summary: 뇌과학에서의 기초적인 연구 내용과 더불어 세계적 최근 연구 동향을 학부학생들에게 알기 쉽게 소개한다. 이를 통하여 학생들이 학부 때에 학습한 수학, 물리, 화학, 생물, 공학 및 컴퓨팅의 지식들이 함께 어우러져서 어떻게 뇌과학에 융합적으로 적용되는 지에 대한 통찰력을 기를 수 있도록 한다. 궁극적으로는 뇌과학 전반에 대해 학생들이 궁금증을 가지고 상상력을 발휘할 수 있는 동기를 부여한다.
syllabus.policies: 강의 중 컴퓨터, 핸드폰 등의 사용은 해당 강의의 학습에 직결된 용도로 스스로 제한하여 집중도를 높일 수 있도록 한다.

syllabus.summary: This course explores the molecular, cellular, and circuitry mechanisms that underlie the function of the mammalian nervous system, with special emphasis on the membrane potential, electrical signaling and synaptic organization. This course will include:    - Surveying the molecular and cellular mechanisms of neuronal communication.    - Coversion channels in excitable membrane, synaptic transmission, and synaptic plasticity.    - Correlation of the properties of ion channels and synaptic transmission with their physiological function such as learning and memory.    - Discussion of the organizational principles for the formation of functional neural networks at synaptic and cellular levels.
syllabus.policies: Laptops/tablets should only be used for course-related activities.

10주차:The metabotropic GABAb receptors, metabotropic glutamate receptors

syllabus.summary: This course explores the relationship between biological and artificial intelligence through direct comparison. In the first half of the semester, students study five major comparison components: representation, learning rules, credit assignment, action selection and control, and efficiency/hardware. In the second half, students gain hands-on experience with spiking neural networks using snnTorch. In the final part, students critically evaluate review literature in neuroscience-inspired AI through solo or group presentations.
syllabus.policies: Letter grade (ABCDF)
schedule: 13주차:Surrogate Gradient Descent in a Convolutional SNN

syllabus.summary: The concepts of limit, derivative, and integral of single-variable functions are extended to multivariate functions and their applications. And this course deals with the basic concepts of 3 by 3 matrices, solving simultaneous ordinary differential equations using matrices, defining multiple integrals, understanding the physics of vector field integration, learning theories such as Stokes' theorem and divergence theorem, and learning Maxwell's equations. Also deals with how to derive fluid dynamics equations by applying the integral theorems.

6주차:maxima and minima-Taylor theorem, extrema of functions

syllabus.summary: In this course we will cover concepts and applications of differentiation and integration of real multivariable functions and vector analysis. Topics will include: elements of Euclidean geometry, limits and continuity of multivariable functions, differentiability, gradients and directional derivatives, higher derivatives, maxima and minima, vector fields, divergence and curl, multiple integrals, path and surface integrals, Green, Stokes and Gauss theorems.

syllabus.summary: Differential equations represent a fundamental mathematical tool used by the whole of science to model physical phenomena: they express how a certain quantity of interest is related to its derivatives. Solutions to such problems are given by differentiable functions, which can be used to provide estimates and predict the evolution of the physical system one is studying. In this course students will learn how to solve several types of ordinary differential equations and also how to investigate and interpret the results.
syllabus.materials: Lecture notes and the recordings of the lectures will be made available on LMS.
syllabus.policies: In order to pass the course students are expected to score at least 20% on both the midterm and the final. In particular, not attending either the midterm or the final will result in a failing grade. Exceptions to this general rule will be granted on a case-by-case basis at the discretion of the instructor.   Make-up exams will be granted only in exceptional cases, following the submission of appropriate documentation explaining the circumstances.  Cheating and plagiarism will not be tolerated and, if discovered, will lead to failing the entire course. This also applies to students who knowingly allow their work to be copied.
schedule: 3주차:Euler’s method, numerical solutions to differential equations
6주차:Existence and uniqueness of solutions to IVP

syllabus.summary: This course introduces the fundamental concepts of electromagnetism in an accessible and intuitive manner. Topics include electric forces and fields, electric potential, electric current, resistance, and basic electrical circuits. Students will explore magnetic fields, magnetic forces, and electromagnetic induction, with emphasis on practical applications. The course concludes with an introduction to electromagnetic waves, highlighting their properties, behavior, and role in the transmission of energy.
syllabus.policies: Attendance is mandatory and will be formally graded. Assignments and exams must be submitted on time. Late submissions will result in point deductions that may affect your final grade. If you anticipate a conflict with a due date or encounter difficulties with an assignment, you must plan ahead and notify the instructor in advance. This will allow us to discuss the situation and, if appropriate, make alternative arrangements.
schedule: 1주차:Static Electricity; Electric Charge and its Conservation; Insulators and Conductors; Induced Charge; Coulomb’s Law; The Electric Field
2주차:Electric Field of a Continuous Charge Distribution; Electric Field Lines; Electric Fields and conductors; Motion of a Charged Particle in an Electric Field; Electric Dipoles

3주차:Electric Potential Energy and Potential Difference; Relation Between Electric Potential and Electric Field; Electric Potential due to Point Charges; Potential Due to Any Charge Distribution; Equipotential Lines and Surfaces; Applications of Electrostatics
4주차:Definition of Capacitance; Calculation of Capacitance; Capacitors in Series and Parallel; Storage of Electrical Energy; Dielectrics; An Atomic Description of Dielectrics
5주차:Electric Current; Ohm’s Law: Resistance and Resistors; Resistivity; Model for Electrical Conduction; Resistance and Temperature; Superconductors; Electrical Power
7주차:EMF and Terminal Voltage; Resistors in Series and Parallel; Kirchhoff’s Rules; EMFs in Series and Parallel; Charging a Battery; RC Circuits – Resistor and Capacitor in Series

8주차:Magnets and Magnetic Fields; Electric Current Produce Magnetic Fields; Force on an Electric Current in a Magnetic Field: Definition of B; Force on an Electric Charge Moving in a Magnetic Field; Torque on a Current Loop

syllabus.summary: This course introduces the fundamental concepts of electromagnetism in an accessible and intuitive manner. Topics include electric forces and fields, electric potential, electric current, resistance, and basic electrical circuits. Students will explore magnetic fields, magnetic forces, and electromagnetic induction, with emphasis on practical applications. The course concludes with an introduction to electromagnetic waves, highlighting their properties, behavior, and role in the transmission of energy.

syllabus.summary: While classical mechanics deals with the motion of mass-bearing particles, electromagnetism broadly covers the motion of charged particles and systems of particles. You will learn about electric fields created by stationary charges, magnetic fields created by charges moving at a constant velocity, and electromagnetic waves created by accelerating charges. The course addresses the motion of charges and magnetic moments within these fields, as well as the properties of elements that make up electric circuits. To understand light, the ray model, wave model, and photon model of light will be examined, alongside topics such as reflection, refraction, interference, diffraction, polarization, and the photoelectric effect.

syllabus.summary: 물리학은 실험과학이다. 실험을 통해서 확인되지 않는 지식은 과학지식이라고 하기 어렵다. 자신이 수행한 실험을 통해 일반물리II 강의를 통해서 배운 지식의 진위를 시험해 보자. 실험 과정 하나하나를 점검해 가면서 자신의 실험과정과 결과에 대해 과학적으로 설명할 수 있어야 한다. 실험을 하고 그것을 통해 과학지식을 확인하는 과정은 물리 뿐만 아니라 모든 실험 과학에서 적용할 수 있다. 실험결과와 여러분이 배운 이론에 바탕 한 계산 값이 맞지 않을 수 있다. 여기에서 새롭고 재미있는 과학이 시작된다. 전자기학 및 광학과 관련된 실험을 통하여 물리 현상을 이해해 본다.
syllabus.policies: 실험에 적극적으로 참여한다. 토론에 적극적으로 참여한다. 개인 위생 및 보건 관련 의무 사항을 준수한다.

syllabus.summary: This course introduces experimental topics in electricity, magnetism, and optics. Students investigate electrostatic and magnetostatic phenomena, DC and AC circuits, and the properties of light as an electromagnetic wave. Through hands-on experiments and discussions, they develop an understanding of fundamental electromagnetic principles and their applications. The course culminates in creative team projects in which students design and conduct experiments on self-selected topics to explore innovative applications of electromagnetism.
syllabus.policies: Students are expected to actively participate in all laboratory activities. Students are expected to actively engage in discussions and contribute to the exchange of ideas. Students must comply with all requirements and guidelines related to personal hygiene and health.
schedule: 6주차:Solenoid Magnetic Field and the Principle of a Transformer

syllabus.summary: 화학은 물질의 정체와 변환을 연구하는 자연과학의 핵심 분야로, 원자와 분자를 이용하여 새로운 화합물을 합성하며 다양한 화학 반응의 특성을 연구하는 분야이다. 학문적으로는 자연과 인간의 생명 현상을 이해하고 더 나은 삶을 위한 관련 첨단과학을 선도하며 산업적으로는 인류의 삶에 필요한 다양한 소재와 에너지를 공급해주고 있다. 실험으로 얻어진 결과와 이론적으로 예측된 값을 비교하여 화학적 사고확장을 유도한다.

syllabus.policies: 사전 보고서를 통해 실험 전 내용을 숙지하고 조별로 실험하며 얻어진 결과는 함께 논의 하되 보고서는 개별 제출한다.

syllabus.summary: General Chemistry Laboratory I introduces fundamental laboratory techniques in chemistry. Students are expected to review safety precautions before each experiment. Throughout the course, students will perform prearranged experimental procedures, work collaboratively with classmates, and accurately collect and analyze experimental data. Emphasis is placed on proper laboratory practices and safety awareness to minimize accidents and maintain a safe learning environment.
syllabus.policies: Students must complete a preliminary report to review pre-lab content before each experiment. Experiments and result discussions must be conducted in groups, but reports must be submitted individually. Proper lab clothing is required. Wearing capes, flip-flops, sandals, or other open-toed shoes is prohibited.

syllabus.summary: Molecular biology and bioinformatics experiments and experiments essential for modern life science research Understand the basics of modern molecular biology experiments by performing practical exercises. In particular, students practice scientific methodology of biological research and experiments encompassing microbiology, molecular biology, and bioinformatics through an actual molecular biology project consisting of several modules.

syllabus.summary: This laboratory course introduces students to fundamental techniques and experimental approaches in biology, reinforcing core concepts through hands-on investigation. Students will conduct experiments in areas such as microscopy, cell biology, enzyme activity, genetics, and molecular biology. Emphasis is placed on developing skills in hypothesis formulation, experimental design, data collection and analysis, and scientific communication. The course complements the associated lecture by providing experiential learning opportunities that highlight the process of scientific inquiry and the application of biological principles in real-world contexts.
syllabus.policies: Laptops/tablets should only be used for course-related activities.

syllabus.summary: '일반생물학 실험'은 현대 생명과학 연구에 필수적인 분자생물학과 생물정보학 실험 및 실습을 수행한다. 특히, 2개의 연구 프로젝트를 진행하여 생물학 연구의 과학적 방법론 및 미생물학, 분자생물학, 생물정보학을 포괄하는 실험을 수행하고 이를 통하여 현대 생물학 연구의 기초를 학습한다.
syllabus.policies: 지도 불응, 안전관리 소홀 및 수업 분위기 방해가 되는 일체의 행동 시 즉시 퇴실조치한다.
schedule: 4주차:유전형X표현형 분석 프로젝트: 세포 genomic DNA 추출
5주차:유전형X표현형 분석 프로젝트: PCR 원리 및 실습
6주차:유전형X표현형 분석 프로젝트: DNA 전기영동 및 정제
10주차:미생물 동정 프로젝트: 미생물 배양용 배지 제조 및 pH 미터 적정 실습
11주차:미생물 동정 프로젝트: 단계희석 및 고체배지 도말
12주차:미생물 동정 프로젝트: CFU 측정 및 colony PCR

15주차:염기서열 분석 결과 분반 공유 및 질의응답

syllabus.summary: This course provides an introduction to fundamental biological principles, emphasizing the molecular and cellular basis of life, genetics, evolution, and the structure-function relationships in living organisms. Topics include the chemistry of life, cell structure and function, metabolism, DNA replication and expression, Mendelian and molecular genetics, and evolutionary processes. The course is designed for students pursuing studies in life sciences, medicine, neuroscience or related fields and provides the foundational knowledge required for advanced coursework in biology.
syllabus.policies: Cell phones should be turned off or set to silent mode during class.  No texting or browsing on phones during class.  Laptops/tablets should only be used for course-related activities.

syllabus.summary: This course provides an integrated introduction to animal and plant biology, evolution, and ecology. Students will learn how animals maintain homeostasis through coordinated physiological systems, how plants grow, reproduce, and respond to their environment, and how evolutionary processes generate biological diversity. The course also examines ecological interactions at the levels of populations, communities, and ecosystems. Emphasis is placed on understanding fundamental biological principles and applying them to real-world biological phenomena.

syllabus.summary: General Chemistry I course introduces foundational concepts in chemistry, focusing on the structure and properties of matter. Students will explore atomic theory, periodic trends, chemical bonding, molecular geometry, and the principles governing chemical reactions and stoichiometry. The course also covers gases, liquids, solids, and intermolecular forces, providing a solid foundation in fundamental chemistry concepts.
syllabus.policies: Using cellphone during lecture and exam is prohibited.

syllabus.summary: General Chemistry II builds upon the fundamental principles introduced in General Chemistry I by examining the behavior of chemical systems in greater depth. Students will explore the properties of solutions, chemical kinetics, chemical equilibrium, acid-base equilibria, thermodynamics, electrochemistry, nuclear chemistry, and the chemistry of nonmetals and transition metals. The course emphasizes the factors that govern chemical reactions, energy transformations, and equilibrium processes, while highlighting applications in environmental, industrial, biological, and technological contexts.

syllabus.summary: Single variable Calculus is an introductory course in the field of calculus, which is a branch of mathematics that deals with the concepts of change and motion. The course is designed to provide students with a solid foundation in the fundamental principles of calculus, preparing them for more advanced mathematical and scientific studies.
syllabus.policies: 반드시 연습시간을 수강 해야함. 연습시간에는 매트랩 실습, 연습문제 풀이, 학생의 질문 등으로 이루어지며, 실습 등을 수행할 때 조교가 진행할 수 있음  질의 및 면담 시간: 강의시간에 별도 공지함. TA 면담 시간은 연습 시 간에 별도 공지   5번까지 결석 허용. 6회 이상(20 %) 정당한 사유 없이 무단 결석한 경우, 본래보다 낮은 학점이 부여될 수 있음. (강의 시작 15분 이후 입실할 경우 지각으로 정의하고. 지각 3번은 결석 1번으로 정의하며. 결석에는 경조사 및 천재지변 등을 모두 포함함. 5회 미만일 경우 따로 교수에게 알릴 필요 없음)   연습 시간에 대한 출석도 위와 동일하게 적용하되 결석 회수는 3회까지 허용(시험 주간 제외20%)    절대 평가.   중간, 기말 고사 중 하나를 치르지 않으면 점수와 관계없이 F 처리됨.  100점 만점 환산 20점(절대적 점수는 아니고 시험의 난이도에 따라 약간의 차이 있음)미만은 F    학생이 피할 수 없는 유고 치 못할 경우 명확한 서류에 의하여 증명되고 담당교수가 인정하는 한에서 학생이 취득한 점수를 고려하여 합리적으로 점수를 부여할 수 있음.   매주 일정한 분량의 숙제(HW)가 주어지고, 숙제는 정해진 제출기한까지 LMS 과제물 게시판 또는 지정된 장소에 제출.    교과 내용과  Matlab 교육 내용을 각각  2번의  퀴즈를 통해 테스트함. 교과 퀴즈 및  Matlab  퀴즈는 아래  강의 계획서에  계획된 일자에  진행하며 원칙적으로 모든 시험은(퀴즈 포함) 재시험 없음.

syllabus.summary: This course provides an introduction to probability and statistical inference with an emphasis on fundamental concepts and their mathematical foundations. Topics include probability models, random variables and their distributions, joint and conditional distributions, sampling distributions, estimation, hypothesis testing, and simple linear regression. The course integrates theoretical development with illustrative examples and applications to data.

syllabus.summary: The objective of the Physical Chemistry 3 course is to extend the fundamental concepts introduced in Physical Chemistry 1 and 2 and to deepen students’ understanding of the behavior of molecules in various states: solids, liquids, and gases. In particular, students will learn essential concepts of reaction kinetics and reaction dynamics, which are crucial for understanding a wide range of chemical and biological catalytic reactions. Students will also explore practical experimental and theoretical methods used to investigate reaction mechanisms. Because this course builds substantially on the material covered in Physical Chemistry 1 and 2, prior completion of these courses is strongly recommended.

syllabus.summary: This course provides a systematic introduction to the mathematical foundations of computer science. Building on mathematical preliminaries such as set theory, logic, and induction, the course covers three core topics. First, students study finite automata, regular expressions, and the properties of regular languages to understand the simplest computational models. Second, context-free languages and pushdown automata are explored to describe the grammatical structure of programming languages. Third, students examine the origins of computer science and study Turing machines — the theoretical model underlying modern computers — in both standard and extended forms, gaining insight into the nature of computability, undecidability (including the Halting Problem), and computational complexity through P, NP, and NP-completeness theory.

syllabus.summary: For the last few decades, microelectronics industry has shown tremendous innovation and improvements. The ground-breaking innovations came along with the advancements of electronic devices. It enabled mobile electronic systems, and allowing analog/digital logic circuit implementations for various applications such as signal processing, optoelectronic devices, sensors, and photovoltaic cells.  In this course, first, we will start by reviewing fundamentals of semiconductor physics. Then we will study electronic devices based on semiconductor materials in detail. After that, we will study modern semiconductor devices such as FinFET. At last,  applications of the semiconductor devices in memory devices will be studied.
syllabus.policies: Attendance is highly recommended.  Active participation in the lecture is encouraged.

syllabus.summary: This course covers advanced knowledge related to analog integrated circuit, including MOSFET-based single and multiple stage single-ended and differential amplifiers, operational amplifiers, feedback analysis, frequency domain analysis and stability, and electronic noise.   The first part of the course reviews transistors, single-stage amplifiers, linearization, and transconductance. Basic differential amplifier structures are introduced next with DC and AC analysis, as well as important amplifier specifications such as CMRR, input/output ranges, and slew-rate. Then, introductory level of negative feedback and stability concepts including phase and gain margins will be provided. Lastly, we will study sources of noise and how it affect the overall signal-to-noise ratio.

syllabus.summary: This course provides an in-depth exploration of digital image processing techniques and their applications. Fundamental concepts such as image representation, enhancement, restoration, reconstruction, and segmentation are covered. Students will gain hands-on experience with modern image processing algorithms and techniques, applying theoretical knowledge to real-world problems. The course will also emphasize the mathematical foundations behind digital image operations, preparing students for both academic and industry careers in fields such as computer vision, machine learning, biomedical imaging, and multimedia applications.

syllabus.summary: OECD(1999)에서는 기업가(entrepreneur)란 경제에서 변화와 성장의 주체가 되며, 혁신적인 아이디어의 생성, 확산, 활용을 가속하여 자원의 효율적 활용을 도모하고 경제활동의 범위를 확장하는 하는 자로 정의하고 있다. 기업가정신은 이러한 기업가의 규범적 가치로 변화와 혁신에 필연적으로 수반하는 불확실성(uncertainty)을 부담하는 것을 의미한다(Knight 1961). 본 과목은 기업가정신의 이론과 실제 및 기술혁신에 관련한 기업경영 전반에 걸친 핵심 논점을 학습함으로써 기술창업의 성공잠재력을 제고하고자 한다.
syllabus.policies: 결석과 지각은 사전 통보를 필수로 함. 무단결석과 지각은 감점요인이 될 것임, 노트북 사용 금지, 핸드폰 사용 금지
schedule: 4주차:실리콘밸리, what it is, what it is not, 기업가와 기업가정신
5주차:혁신의 이해, 석기에서 AI까지 만들어낸 인간의 생각
6주차:도구 발전은 “새로운 착상”의 역사“새로운 착상”의 원천 : 수렴적 사고와 발산적 사고의 콜라보
7주차:도구를 만드는 도구 – 과학과 기술   Science Push vs. Technology Push   과학은 혁명적(revolution)으로 변화하지만 기술은 진화(evolution)한다.   기술 패러다임과 기술 궤적(Technological Trajectories)
10주차:혁신이란 무엇인가? 경영과 혁신, 혁신의 촉발 요인
11주차:혁신의 문화, 혁신의 유형, Big Bang Innovation, 신속결합혁신

syllabus.summary: This course is designed to help students develop the English skills needed for academic and professional purposes. It aims to provide students with an opportunity to familiarize themselves with the use of English in a variety of stimulating topics. Students will work towards improving their ability to work with the de facto language of international academia: English.
syllabus.policies: Attendance Policy: All classes are mandatory and must be attended. If you are unable to attend class, you must inform your professor at least 24 hours prior to your intended absence. If you have a reasonable excuse for your absence you will be given an excused absence. An excused absence does not negatively affect your final grade, however repeated absences (excused or not) may result in a warning from your professor, after which any further absences may lead to being awarded a failing or incomplete grade in the course. If you miss class due to ill-health, provide a medical certificate to your professor on the first class meeting after your return to receive your excused absence. If you are absent without a reasonable excuse, you will be marked absent from the class meeting. If you are marked absent 4 or more times during the semester you will receive a failing grade for the course. Please arrive on time for all class meetings. Three late arrivals will result in being marked as an unexcused absence. Arriving more than 20 minutes after the start of class will automatically be marked as an unexcused absence. / Late Work Policy: There are no make-ups for work completed in class, such as group work or presentations. Any work not completed during class time must be finished and turned in by the due date set by your professor. If you are absent (even if you have a reasonable excuse), it is your responsibility to find out what work was assigned during the classes you missed and to complete that work by the due date. Failure to submit work by the due date is unacceptable, and the work will not receive any feedback. / Group Work Policy: Everyone must take part in group projects. For daily work throughout the semester, you will be assigned to a group. All members of a group will receive the same score. In other words, the assignment is given 1 score, and everyone in the group receives it. For larger assignments and projects, you may work in groups but be assessed individually. / Student/Professor Communication: If you have a question regarding the course administration or course content you should: Ask your professor during or after class. Because it is common for many students to have the same questions, asking in class allows every student to get the answer at the same time. Ask your professor via email if you have a private question. Your professor will designate time each week specifically for helping students. Your professor will post an appointment sheet on or near their office door. To make an appointment, sign up for the time you wish to visit. Alternatively, you can send an email to your professor to make an appointment. Outside of office hours, your professor may be busy attending to other duties, so they may not be available to meet.

An excused absence does not negatively affect your final grade, however repeated absences (excused or not) may result in a warning from your professor, after which any further absences may lead to being awarded a failing or incomplete grade in the course. If you miss class due to ill-health, provide a medical certificate to your professor on the first class meeting after your return to receive your excused absence. If you are absent without a reasonable excuse, you will be marked absent from the class meeting. If you are marked absent 4 or more times during the semester you will receive a failing grade for the course. Please arrive on time for all class meetings. Three late arrivals will result in being marked as an unexcused absence. Arriving more than 20 minutes after the start of class will automatically be marked as an unexcused absence. / Late Work Policy: There are no make-ups for work completed in class, such as group work or presentations. Any work not completed during class time must be finished and turned in by the due date set by your professor. If you are absent (even if you have a reasonable excuse), it is your responsibility to find out what work was assigned during the classes you missed and to complete that work by the due date. Failure to submit work by the due date is unacceptable, and the work will not receive any feedback. / Group Work Policy: Everyone must take part in group projects. For daily work throughout the semester, you will be assigned to a group. All members of a group will receive the same score. In other words, the assignment is given 1 score, and everyone in the group receives it. For larger assignments and projects, you may work in groups but be assessed individually. / Student/Professor Communication: If you have a question regarding the course administration or course content you should: Ask your professor during or after class. Because it is common for many students to have the same questions, asking in class allows every student to get the answer at the same time. Ask your professor via email if you have a private question. Your professor will designate time each week specifically for helping students. Your professor will post an appointment sheet on or near their office door. To make an appointment, sign up for the time you wish to visit. Alternatively, you can send an email to your professor to make an appointment. Outside of office hours, your professor may be busy attending to other duties, so they may not be available to meet.

syllabus.summary: As science and technology play an increasingly significant role in shaping contemporary society, it is essential to develop a critical understanding of their nature and impacts. This course aims to cultivate students' ability to examine the characteristics and social implications of modern science and technology from multiple perspectives by introducing major theoretical discussions in Science and Technology Studies (STS), an interdisciplinary field that integrates diverse approaches from the humanities and social sciences. Building on these theoretical foundations, students will engage in discussions of contemporary issues and debates related to science and technology.
syllabus.policies: The course is designed for both Korean and international students and is conducted primarily in English. While course materials and lectures are provided mainly in English, the use of English and Korean in lectures, discussions, and question-and-answer sessions may be adjusted according to the linguistic backgrounds and needs of the enrolled students.
schedule: 1주차:Introduction to Science and Technology Studies: Key Questions in STS
2주차:The Emergence of Sociology of Scientific Knowledge
4주차:Contemporary Science in Practice: Screening and discussion of <Naturally Obsessed>
6주차:Screening and discussion of <Who Killed the Electric Car?>
7주차:Risk Governance in the Era of Post-Normal Science
9주차:Emerging Technology in Everyday Life: Screening and discussion of <In the Family>
12주차:Academic, Social, and Ethical Dimensions of Scientists’ Work
14주차:Group Presentations: Book Review and Critical Analysis

syllabus.policies: - 중간고사는 01, 02, 03, 04, 05분반 공통 논술형 지필 고사로 시행하며, 기말고사는 팀 프로젝트 결과물로 대체한다.  - 팀 프로젝트 수업 중 토론과 상호 논평(peer review)이 진행되며, 이를 수업 참여도 성적에 반영한다.  - office hour를 이용하여 수업 및 과제에 대한 개별 상담을 받을 수 있다.   - 과제물은 기한 내에 제출해야 하며, 기한을 넘긴 과제물은 감점을 받는다.  - 과제물 제출은 LMS 과제함 제출을 원칙으로 하며, 제출 여부를 반드시 확인하여 불이익이 없도록 한다.

syllabus.summary: <글로벌문화와 의사소통Introduction to Intercultural Communication>은 문화와 문화 간 의사소통에 대한 다양한 관점과 사례를 검토함으로써 1)자기 자신(의 문화)에 대한 이해를 돕고, 나아가 2)성공적인 문화간 소통에 필요한 언어적, 문화적 감수성과 실천 의지를 기르는 것을 목적으로 한다. 문화를 특정 민족/국가의 고유한 특성으로 간주하는 본질주의적 시각에 대한 비판적 검토와 더불어 문화의 역동적, 역사적, 정치적, 담론적인 성격에 주목한다. 이를 위해 전반부에 문화에 대한 차원적인 이해 관점에서 출발하여 Adrian Holliday의 문화간 의사소통 이론을 집중적으로 살핀다. 학기 후반부에는 ‘국제공용어로서의 영어(학습)’과 ‘해외연수’ 등 한국 대학생의 삶과 밀접한 관계가 있는 주제를 문화 간 의사소통의 관점에서 탐색한다. 마지막 4주 동안은 ‘디지스트 커뮤니티에 존재하는 문화적 갈등/충돌과 해결방안’을 주제로 ‘문제중심학습(PBL, Problem Based Learning)’을 활용, 학생들 스스로 문제를 정의하고 탐색하여 해결책을 제안하는 프로젝트를 진행함으로써, 문화간 의사소통의 이론과 실제에 대한 이해의 깊이를 더한다.

syllabus.summary: 본 과목은 1학년 신입생들이 대학 생활에 성공적으로 적응하고 자신의 진로와 전공을 설계할 수 있도록 지원하는 데 목적을 둔다. 특히 진로탐색 및 전공설계 I에서는 학교생활 안내, 트랙별 교과 및 진로 설명, 멘토링 제도 등을 통해 학생들이 학교 자원과 네트워크를 효과적으로 활용하도록 돕고, 각 트랙의 학문적 특성을 이해함으로써 다양한 학문적 선택지를 탐색할 수 있도록 하며, 저명한 학자를 초청한 특별 세미나를 통해 학생들의 학문적 시야를 넓히게 한다.

syllabus.summary: This course aims to introduce the causes, background, and historical development of South Korea’s foreign policy decisions. In particular, it focuses on discussing and analyzing South Korea’s security, trade, and unification policies, as well as its public diplomacy, in the post-Korean War era.
syllabus.materials: Eui Kon Kim, Korea and International Relations, INHA Center of K-Academic expansion project (https://www.kmooc.kr)
syllabus.policies: Course Participation: Students are required to complete an online lecture before attending the bi-weekly in-class discussion session. Active participation in discussions is expected. If you exceed one absences, your grade will be treated as a failure.

syllabus.summary: 21세기는 과학기술의 시대이다. 이제는 과학기술이 없는 삶을 상상할 수 없으며, 과학기술의 특징과 그 사회적 함의를 이해하는 것은 시민으로서 갖추어야 할 필수 자질이라고 할 수 있다. 현재 우리의 삶에 영향을 미치는 다양한 과학기술의 기원, 의미, 역할, 한계 등을 이해하기 위해서는 먼저 그것이 발전해 온 역사를 살펴볼 필요가 있다. 본 수업에서는 고대, 중세, 과학 혁명기를 거쳐 근대과학과 현대과학에 이르기까지 과학사의 긴 여정을 개괄적으로 살펴봄으로써, 과학기술이 어떤 과정을 통해 인류 문명에서 핵심적인 지위를 차지하게 되었는지를 이해할 것이다.
syllabus.materials: 본 수업의 목적은 과학기술의 발전 과정에 대한 이해에 기반하여 수강생들로 하여금 현대 과학기술의 특징과 사회적 함의를 비판적으로 성찰할 수 있게끔 만드는 것이며, 이를 효율적으로 달성하기 위한 과제들이 마련되어 있다. (1) 강의 내용에 대한 이해를 평가하기 위해 중간시험, 기말시험을 실시한다. 간단한 서술식 문항들에 대해 정해진 시간 안에 스스로 답안지를 작성하여 제출. (2) 수강생들은 학기 중 과학사 및 관련 인접 분야(과학학 전반)의 책을 조별로 1권 골라 함께 읽고 수업 시간에 발표한다. 책 선정 방식 및 서평 제출일자는 수업 시간에 설명할 예정. (3) 수강생들은 몇몇 주차에 수업 시간 내 이루어지는 조별 토론에 참여하며, 토론 결과를 간략히 정리하여 발표하여야 한다.  * 성적 평가 비율: 출석 10%, 중간시험 30%, 기말시험 30%, 조별 과제 30%

syllabus.summary: 이 강좌에서는 동서양의 지성사에 큰 영향을 미친 고전 텍스트를 읽고 토론한다. 특히 이번 학기에 다루는 작품들은 유토피아와 디스토피아를 주요 주제로 삼고 있으며, 각 시대의 사상가와 작가들이 인간과 사회, 권력과 질서에 대해 어떠한 문제를 제기하고 어떤 대안을 상상했는지를 살펴본다. 인공지능, 생명공학, 데이터 기술 등 과학기술의 발전이 인간과 사회를 빠르게 변화시키고 있는 오늘날, 우리는 기술이 만들어 갈 미래에 대해 더욱 풍부한 상상력과 비판적 성찰을 요구받고 있다. 이러한 문제의식에 따라 본 강좌에서는 16세기부터 21세기에 이르는 고전 텍스트를 함께 읽으며, 인간, 사회, 권력, 과학기술의 관계를 탐구하고 더 나은 미래에 대한 다양한 비전과 그 한계를 탐색한다. 수업은 학생 중심의 읽기와 토론을 기반으로 진행된다. 학생들은 매주 지정된 분량의 텍스트를 읽고 질문을 작성하며, 수업 시간에는 이를 바탕으로 조별 및 전체 토론을 진행한다. 이를 통해 고전 텍스트를 역사적 산물로 이해하는 데 그치지 않고, 오늘날의 과학기술과 사회가 직면한 문제들을 비판적으로 검토하고자 한다.
syllabus.policies: 출석: 수강생은 강의 일정에 따라 반드시 수업에 참석해야 한다. 결석이 불가피한 사유(예: 가족의 사망과 중병, 수강생 본인의 입원, 천재지변)가 없다면 모두 결석으로 간주한다. 사소한 질병으로 인한 결석을 포함해서 결석이 2회를 초과하면 F학점을 부여한다. 일반적인 질병으로 인한 외래 진료 확인서를 받지 않는다.

syllabus.summary: The theme of this course for Fall 2026 is Science, Technology, and Society. The course has two main objectives: (1) to improve students’ English language skills and (2) to explore the socio-political implications of science and technology through reading and discussing science fiction in English. From a language-learning perspective, the course is grounded in the view of language as a social practice. Rather than studying English in isolation, students will engage with literary texts that present authentic social situations, conflicts, and perspectives. Through reading, discussion, and writing, students will develop their ability to understand and use English in meaningful contexts. In terms of content, science fiction provides a valuable lens through which to examine the relationship between science, technology, and society. By imagining technologically advanced futures, science fiction invites readers to reflect on the social, political, and economic consequences of scientific and technological change. Throughout the semester, students will critically examine these issues while considering their own future roles as scientists and engineers. Students will read three to four science fiction texts: one short story and two to three novelettes or novels. The texts will be selected by students from a shortlist during the first week of the semester.

syllabus.summary: 현재 대한민국을 비롯한 세계 각국에서는 민주주의의 위기를 경험하고 있다. 대표적인 예로 극우세력의 준동과 포퓰리즘의 저변 확대는 정치적 양극화로 인한 대의 민주주의의 퇴행에서 비롯된 현상이다. 정당은 대의 민주주의의 핵심제도로서 민주주의의 위기는 곧 정당의 위기로 치환될 수 있다. 따라서 한국 민주주의의 실제를 정당정치의 관점에서 체계적으로 분석하고 비판적으로 성찰하는 일은 지속가능한 민주주의를 모색하는데 있어서 필수적인 것이다. 따라서 대한민국 제1공화국 시기부터 현재까지의 정치사와 정당구도에 대한 이해가 필수적이며, 정당의 분류와 기능, 정당조직, 선거제도와 정당체제, 이념, 후보선택, 선거 캠페인, 정책형성, 정부와의 관계와 같은 정당에 대한 이론적 관점들을 명확하게 이해하는 것이 필요하다. 이 과정에서 한국 민주주의의 역사와 정당정치의 여러 문제들을 인식함으로써 학생들이 궁극적으로 정당정치의 본질에 대해서 생각해볼 수 있는 기회를 가질 수 있기를 기대한다.

syllabus.summary: This course aims to help students understand the characteristics of global regions and develop the ability to analyze them from a geographical perspective. In an era of globalization that demands creative thinking, the course examines the diverse economic, social, and cultural interactions that shape regions around the world, as well as the natural environments in which they exist. Drawing on foundational concepts from the social sciences, the course fosters a broad understanding of cultural and spatial diversity through the study of world geography.

syllabus.summary: 'Reading Poetry' provides a basic approach to the appreciation and analysis of poetry from thematic, theoretical, and historical perspectives. Throughout the course, students will read, write about, and discuss selected poems and essays on poetics from ancient Greek to contemporary times. Each class will consist of an oral presentation, group discussion, and lecture. As the title suggests, students may recite several lines from poems. By the end of the semester, students can expect to have developed a deeper understanding of poetry, and to have it become a part of their lives.

syllabus.summary: The Scientific Writing course is designed to help students understand the fundamentals of effective scientific and technical writing and write a mini research paper as the final project. To this end, the course consists of two phases: Lecture/Discussion and the Writing Project. In the first quarter of the semester, students will learn the basics of academic research paper writing such as ethics and plagiarism, paraphrasing, referencing, and style; they will also explore the structure of scientific writing and discursive features specific to scientific writing by surveying published research papers in the science and technology fields. Equipped with this basic understanding about writing a research paper, students will move on to learn how to compose each of the sections of a research paper centering on content, frequently used vocabulary, discourse-organizing words, and syntax patterns. As they learn the specifics of each section, students will have ample opportunities to practice writing through not only structured class activities but also actually drafting their own research paper section by section according to the class progress.

syllabus.summary: This course is designed to help students understand the fundamentals of effective scientific and technical writing and to produce a short research paper as the final project. The course consists of two main phases: Lecture/Discussion and the Writing Project. In the first part of the semester, students learn the foundations of academic research writing such as research ethics and plagiarism, paraphrasing and referencing, academic style; they will also explore the structure of scientific writing and discursive features specific to scientific writing by surveying published research papers in the science and technology fields. Building on this foundation, students learn how to compose each section of a research paper centering on content development, key vocabulary, discourse organization, and relevant syntax. They draft their own papers step by step and engage in multiple rounds of revision through peer and instructor feedback. A process-oriented approach to writing is emphasized throughout the course. The course also incorporates reflective engagement with digital writing tools. Students may use tools such as machine translation, generative AI, and other writing platforms to support their writing. At the end of the semester, students deliver a brief reflection on their writing process and digital tool use to promote transparency, responsibility, and awareness in academic writing.

syllabus.summary: 분자생물학은 20세기 이후의 현대 생명과학을 형성하는 주요 패러다임을 정립하였다. 그리고, 분자생물학은 '실험과 사유'를 통해 발전한 학문 체계이다. 필연적으로, 분자생물학은 '실험'과 '이론'이 반드시 병행하는 교과목이다.   본 수업은 3학년 전공 필수 교과목으로 운영되는 분자생물학 이론 수업에 발 맞추어, 실험 교과목은 분자생물학 교과서에서도 자세하게 다루는 이론을 실제 프로젝트 수행 과정에서 구현하도록 학습한다. 다만, 모든 실험을 백화점 식으로 나열하는 학습법을 지양하고, 대부분의 생명과학 연구실 및 생명과학 기업에서 활발하게 사용하는 주요 분자생물학 기법을 학습하고자 한다.  즉, 분자생물학 실험 기법을 역사적으로 개괄하되, 현대 생명과학에서도 활발하게 활용되는 실험 기법(예, 클로닝 등)을 실습하고, 단순 체험을 넘어 기법의 주요 과정을 체득할 수 있도록 수업을 구성하였다.
